user_message: Just came out of account health with Tailspin Toys. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-03-01
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Tailspin Toys)

[00:01:15] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:08:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] David (Tailspin Toys): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] David (Tailspin Toys): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF43-I trap (Duplicates existing opportunity at account); produces complete BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes, opportunity proposal with stage/value/close-date, confi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF43-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF43-I failure mode (Duplicates existing opportunity at account)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF43-I -- Duplicates existing opportunity at account
